questions.question: Which SVG element is used to draw a circle?
questions.explanation: The <circle> element draws a circle and requires cx, cy (center coordinates) and r (radius) attributes. The <ellipse> element is for ovals with separate rx and ry radii.

questions.question: What does the viewBox attribute on an <svg> element define?
choices: The background color of the SVG
The coordinate system and visible area of the SVG content
The maximum file size of the SVG
The animation speed for SVG elements
questions.explanation: The viewBox attribute defines the position and dimensions of the SVG viewport in the format "min-x min-y width height". It establishes the internal coordinate system and controls how the SVG content scales.

questions.question: Which SVG element uses a d attribute containing drawing commands like M, L, and Z?
questions.explanation: The <path> element uses the d (data) attribute with commands like M (move to), L (line to), C (curve to), and Z (close path) to define complex shapes and curves.

questions.question: What is a key advantage of inline SVG (placed directly in HTML) over using an tag to load an SVG file?
choices: Inline SVG loads faster because it avoids an HTTP request
Inline SVG elements can be styled with CSS and manipulated with JavaScript
Inline SVG automatically scales to fill the browser window
Inline SVG supports raster image formats
questions.explanation: When SVG is inline in the HTML, each element is part of the DOM and can be targeted with CSS selectors, event listeners, and JavaScript manipulation. An SVG loaded via is treated as a single opaque image.

questions.question: The SVG element used to draw a rectangle is ___.
questions.explanation: The <rect> element draws a rectangle and uses x, y, width, and height attributes to define its position and size. Optional rx and ry attributes create rounded corners.

questions.question: The SVG <polygon> element's vertices are defined by the ___ attribute.
questions.explanation: The points attribute contains a list of x,y coordinate pairs that define each vertex of the polygon. The browser automatically closes the shape by connecting the last point to the first.
